description: geographical and cultural region which extends over a part of France and the British Channel Islands
description_wiki: Normandy is a geographical and cultural region in northwestern Europe, roughly coextensive with the historical Duchy of Normandy.

description: 1949 original cast recording starring Mary Martin, Ezio Pinza
1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.
Chester William Nimitz (; 24 February 1885 – 20 February 1966) was a fleet admiral in the United States Navy.

description: American politician and 48th Mayor of Chicago
description_wiki: Richard Joseph Daley (May 15, 1902 – December 20, 1976) was an American politician who served as the mayor of Chicago from 1955, and the chairman of the Cook County Democratic Party from 1953, until his death.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

surface_forms: Great Lakes for our Naval training
description: naval training base for the United States Navy, outside of Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: Naval Station Great Lakes (NAVSTA Great Lakes) is the home of the United States Navy's only current boot camp, located near North Chicago, in Lake County, Illinois, along Lake Michigan.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

surface_forms: troop ship they made out of the Queen Mary
description_wiki: RMS Queen Mary is a historic retired British ocean liner that operated primarily on the North Atlantic Ocean from 1936 to 1967 for the Cunard Line.

USS Hornet (CV/CVA/CVS-12) is an Essex-class aircraft carrier built for the United States Navy (USN) during World War II. Completed in late 1943, the ship was assigned to the Fast Carrier Task Force (variously designated as Task Force 38 or 58) in the Pacific Ocean, the navy's primary offensive force during the Pacific War.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.

description: largest island of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States of America
